steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what "practically" means as used in the phrase "a thick curtain of long, coily hair that practically engulfs her." The blank must describe the degree to which the hair covers the girl, not her mood or the hair's behavior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Cheerfully': An adverb of emotion or manner. Hair cannot act cheerfully; the sentence is describing extent of coverage, not a feeling or disposition. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Doubtfully': Means with uncertainty or hesitation. The narrator is making a direct visual observation — there is no doubt or skepticism being expressed. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Almost': Means very nearly but not completely. "Hair that almost engulfs her" captures the image of being nearly swallowed by extremely voluminous hair, which is exactly the degree the passage conveys. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Never': Means at no time. "Hair that never engulfs her" directly contradicts the passage — the entire point of the image is that the hair nearly does engulf her. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
